text.title: Conditional quality gates
text.summary: Items 5, 6, 7 are conditional on what the feature actually touches. This file pins the conditions so the reviewer cannot waive the gate by claiming inapplicability without justification.

rule.statement: If ANY file under the backend source tree changed, item 5 (API tests pass) MUST be ticked.
rule.rationale: Even one line in a serializer can shift a contract. The git diff is the source of truth, not the developer's assessment of scope.

example.note: Feature touches `apps/billing/views.py` → API tests required → reviewer confirms `pytest apps/billing/tests/api/` passed in CI artefact.

rule.statement: If the feature has user-facing flow (page, form, button, command), item 6 requires AT LEAST one positive test case AND one negative test case per flow.
rule.rationale: Positive only is a smoke test. Negative trumps positive for the boring edges — auth failure, network down, invalid input — which is where users actually break the product.

antipattern.description: "Playwright covers it" with one happy-path spec file and no negative cases.
antipattern.reason: Half the bugs hide on the path nobody automated. The pos+neg rule is the cheapest insurance.

rule.statement: If the feature renders or changes any visible UI element, item 7 requires a documented Nielsen 5 + Norman 2 audit in ui-ux-design.md before the box is ticked.
rule.rationale: Heuristic audits cost minutes and catch the silent UX regressions that no unit test sees.

text.reference: Stack-to-gate matrix; consumed when computing which items apply.
